chat_page.title: Chat
chat_page.subtitle: Hermes · streaming · Sprint 2
chat_page.empty_sessions: No sessions yet. Start chatting to create one.
chat_page.empty_adapter_sessions: No {{adapter}} sessions. Switch to “All agents” to see others.
chat_page.message_placeholder: Message Hermes…  (Enter to send, Shift+Enter for newline)
chat_page.hero_subtitle_prefix: Connected through the local gateway on
chat_page.hero_suggestion_1: Explain what Hermes Agent is in one sentence.
chat_page.hero_suggestion_2: Summarize the TRAE sandboxing model in 3 bullets.
chat_page.hero_suggestion_3: Generate a basic Rust Tokio example for me.

models_page.title: Language models
models_page.subtitle: The LLM backing the Hermes agent — configured in ~/.hermes/config.yaml
models_page.change_model_desc: Pick a provider, set the model id, and save. The gateway must be restarted for changes to take effect.

runbooks.title: Runbooks
runbooks.subtitle: Named prompt templates with placeholders. Invoke from the command palette or this page.
runbooks.empty_desc: Create one to reuse a prompt with named placeholders across sessions.
runbooks.no_params_to_fill: This runbook has no parameters — launches the template as-is.

field.name: Name
field.template_hint: — wrap placeholders in double curly braces

scope.no_active_profile: No active Hermes profile — showing universal runbooks only
scope.empty_visible_desc: Toggle "Show all scopes" above, or create a universal runbook.

skills.title: Skills
skills.subtitle: Browse, edit, and save Hermes skill files under ~/.hermes/skills/.
skills.pick_title: Pick a skill on the left
skills.pick_desc: Or click New skill to start one. Files are plain Markdown; Hermes reads them on the next run.
skills.empty_tree: No skills yet. Create one with the New button in the header.

field.path_hint: Relative to ~/.hermes/skills/. Nested paths like work/standup.md are fine.

terminal.title: Terminal
terminal.subtitle: A native PTY-backed shell in your browser. Multi-tab, full UTF-8, resize-aware.
terminal.open_hint: Open a terminal to get started.

trajectory.title: Trajectory
trajectory.subtitle: Visualise a past session as a timeline of messages and tool calls.
trajectory.empty_desc: Send a chat turn and come back — each session renders here with a tool-call timeline.
trajectory.session_empty_desc: This session has no messages yet.

budgets.title: Budgets
budgets.subtitle: Cap spend per scope, period, and breach action. Projection uses local usage history and a built-in price table.
budgets.breached: Over budget — action will fire on the next send.
budgets.empty_desc: Add one to see your projected spend against a cap. All storage is local — no cloud sync.

home.title: Welcome to Corey
home.subtitle: A control plane for AI agents — beautiful, fast, and not locked to one.

analytics.title: Analytics
analytics.subtitle: Usage rolled up from your local session history.
analytics.empty: No activity yet — start a conversation on the Chat page and come back here.

tokens.title: Tokens (last 30 days)
tokens.subtitle: Prompt + completion tokens per day.
tokens.empty: No token usage recorded yet — send a chat turn to start tracking.

feedback.title: Reply feedback
feedback.subtitle: Lifetime 👍 / 👎 ratings left on assistant replies.
feedback.empty: No ratings yet — hover an assistant reply on the Chat page to rate it.

appearance.title: Appearance
appearance.desc: Visual preferences stored locally on this machine.

gateway.title: Gateway
gateway.desc: Where Corey sends chat requests. Changes take effect immediately — no restart needed.
gateway.api_key_hint: Optional. Matches the gateway's API_SERVER_KEY. Stored locally in plaintext.
gateway.api_key_placeholder: (leave empty if the gateway is unauthenticated)

routing_rules.title: Routing rules
routing_rules.desc: When the composed message matches a rule, the next turn is sent to the rule's target adapter instead of whichever one is active. First enabled match in list order wins; an empty list means routing is off.
routing_rules.empty: No rules yet. Add one to route /code, /translate, or other prefixes to the right adapter automatically.
routing_rules.field_id_hint: 1–64 chars: lowercase letters, digits, '-' or '_'. Frozen after save.

hermes_instances.title: Extra Hermes instances
hermes_instances.desc: Register additional Hermes gateways so you can switch between them in the AgentSwitcher. Each entry is registered as 'hermes:<id>' alongside the primary gateway above.
hermes_instances.empty: No extra instances yet. Click "Add instance" to register one.
hermes_instances.field_id_hint: 1–32 chars: lowercase letters, digits, '-' or '_'. Frozen after save (used as the adapter id).
hermes_instances.field_default_model_placeholder: (optional, falls back to the gateway's own default)
hermes_instances.field_sandbox_scope_hint: Per-agent filesystem isolation. Falls back to the default scope if unset.
hermes_instances.confirm_delete: Delete instance "{{id}}"? Its sessions remain in history but can no longer send new turns.

sandbox_scopes.title: Sandbox scopes
sandbox_scopes.desc: Named groups of workspace roots. Assign one to a Hermes instance to restrict what that agent's IPC-originated file ops can touch.
sandbox_scopes.confirm_delete: Delete scope "{{id}}"? Any instance pointed at it will fall back to the default scope.

model.title: Default language model
model.desc: Which LLM the Hermes agent uses when a chat doesn't specify one. Individual chats can override this from the composer.
model.label: Model id

storage.title: Storage
storage.desc: Where your local data lives on disk. Back these up or delete them manually.

sandbox.title: File sandbox
sandbox.desc: Control which directories the app can reach. Corey's disk operations are confined to workspace roots; sensitive system paths (e.g. ~/.ssh) are always denied.
sandbox.mode_dev_allow_hint: First launch. Everything outside the denylist is allowed. Adding a root flips to enforced mode.
sandbox.mode_enforced_hint: Only paths inside an approved workspace root can be touched.

logs.title: Config changelog
logs.subtitle: Every config mutation is journaled. Revert from here.
logs.empty_desc: When you edit the Hermes model or API keys, each change lands here with a Revert button.
logs.not_revertible_env: API key values are never stored, so a deletion can't be undone. Re-add the key manually.

compare.title: Compare models
compare.subtitle: Run one prompt against up to 4 models in parallel. Each lane streams independently.
compare.prompt_placeholder: Type a prompt, then Ctrl/⌘+Enter or Run. Multi-line is fine.
compare.empty_title: Pick at least one model and type a prompt
compare.empty_desc: Each selected model runs the same prompt in parallel. Lanes appear here with streaming output, per-lane latency, and winner highlights.

save_as_skill.disabled_hint: Send at least one message and wait for a reply first.
save_as_skill.title: Save this conversation as a Skill
save_as_skill.subtitle: Writes ~/.hermes/skills/<name>.md so Hermes picks the skill up on its next turn. Edit the body below to distil what should be reusable.
save_as_skill.name: Skill name
save_as_skill.body_hint: Frontmatter + full transcript pre-filled. Trim freely before saving.

skill_hub.cli_missing_desc: The Hermes command-line tool is required to browse and install skills from the hub. Install Hermes Agent from https://hermes-agent.nousresearch.com/ and restart Corey.
skill_hub.install_hint: Full slug including source prefix, e.g. official/security/1password

mcp.title: MCP servers
mcp.subtitle: Model Context Protocol servers Hermes can talk to. Entries live under mcp_servers: in ~/.hermes/config.yaml — Hermes forks stdio commands and connects HTTP URLs itself.
mcp.confirm_delete: Delete MCP server "{{id}}"? Hermes will stop loading its tools on next reload.
mcp.restart_hint: MCP config changed. Hermes needs a reload before the new tools show up.
mcp.empty_desc: Add an MCP server to expose its tools to Hermes. Try the filesystem server first: npx -y @modelcontextprotocol/server-filesystem /path/to/project.
mcp.form_id_locked: Ids are frozen after creation — rename by deleting and re-adding.
mcp.transport_stdio_hint: Hermes forks the command. Use for npm / uvx / cargo MCP servers.
mcp.transport_url_hint: Hermes connects over HTTP. Use for hosted MCP servers.
mcp.form_error_id_dots: Id cannot contain '.' — it would be mis-interpreted as a nested YAML path.
mcp.form_error_must_be_object: Config must be a JSON object.

memory.title: Memory
memory.subtitle: Edit the notes Hermes injects into every prompt. Agent memory lives in ~/.hermes/MEMORY.md; user profile in ~/.hermes/USER.md.
memory.new_file_hint: New file — first save will create it

channels.title: Platform channels
channels.subtitle: Credentials + behavior for every messenger Hermes can talk through.
channels.verified_title: Shipping end-to-end smoke test covers this channel.
channels.not_hot_reloadable: This channel is not hot-reloaded — you'll need to restart the gateway for the change to take effect.
channels.restart_prompt: Saved. Restart the Hermes gateway so the change takes effect now?

weixin.hint_account_id: WeiXin account identifier (the iLink bot account).
weixin.hint_token: iLink access token paired with the account ID.
weixin.hint_base_url: Overrides the default https://ilinkai.weixin.qq.com endpoint. Leave blank unless you're on a forked deployment.
weixin.hint_dm_policy: DM policy: open | allowlist | pairing | disabled. Defaults to 'open'.
weixin.hint_group_policy: Group policy: open | allowlist | disabled. Defaults to 'disabled'.
weixin.hint_allowed_users: Comma-separated WeiXin user IDs allowed to talk to the bot.

whatsapp.hint_enabled: Set to 'true' to enable the WhatsApp bridge process.
whatsapp.hint_mode: 'bot' (standard bot) or 'self-chat' (route to your own WhatsApp account).
whatsapp.hint_allowed_users: Comma-separated phone numbers (+<country><number>) allowed to talk to the bot.
whatsapp.hint_allow_all: Set to 'true' to skip the allowlist (dangerous on public deployments).
whatsapp.mention_patterns: Regex patterns that count as a mention

card_desc.telegram: Telegram group/DM bot. Easiest to configure, most common.
card_desc.discord: Discord server bot. Good for community groups.
card_desc.slack: Slack workspace bot (Socket Mode, needs Bot + App tokens).
card_desc.whatsapp: WhatsApp bridge (unofficial API, requires a separate bridge process).
card_desc.feishu: Feishu/Lark bot. Create an app in the Feishu Open Platform.

discord.hint_token: Discord Developer Portal → Bot → Copy Token.

slack.hint_token: Slack App settings → OAuth & Permissions → Bot User OAuth Token (xoxb-…).
slack.hint_app_token: App-level token (xapp-…) required for Socket Mode. Both tokens are needed for the standard Hermes Slack setup.

matrix.dm_mention_thread: Thread DMs when the bot is @-mentioned

feishu.hint_app_secret: Paired with App ID; keep secret.

wecom.hint_websocket_url: Optional override for the WeCom websocket endpoint. Defaults to wss://openws.work.weixin.qq.com.
wecom.hint_allowed_users: Comma-separated WeCom user IDs allowed to talk to the bot.

profiles.title: Profiles
profiles.subtitle: Each profile is a self-contained Hermes config under ~/.hermes/profiles/.
profiles.missing_desc: Hermes hasn't created this yet. Expected at: {{path}}
profiles.empty_desc: Create one to keep separate configs for prod, dev, or a demo account.
profiles.confirm_delete: Delete profile "{{name}}"? This removes its directory on disk.
profiles.cant_delete_active: Switch to a different profile first.
profiles.import_overwrite_warn: A profile named "{{name}}" already exists. Continuing will replace it on disk.
profiles.activate_confirm_fresh: Set "{{to}}" as the active Hermes profile?
profiles.activate_restart_gateway: Also restart the Hermes gateway so the change takes effect immediately.

hermes_logs.missing_desc: Hermes hasn't written to this log. Expected at: {{path}}

chat.title: No conversations yet
chat.desc: Start a chat session to see it here. Ctrl+Enter sends, Esc cancels a stream.

compare.title: Compare models side by side
compare.desc: Send one prompt to multiple models at once. Coming in Phase 4.

skills.title: Skills will live here
skills.desc: Browse, edit, and version skills. Coming in Phase 4.

trajectory.title: Session trajectory
trajectory.desc: Visualize turns, tool calls, and costs as a timeline. Coming in Phase 4.

analytics.title: Usage analytics
analytics.desc: Token usage, costs, daily trend, model mix. Coming in Phase 2.

logs.title: Agent logs
logs.desc: Tail agent / gateway / error logs with filters. Coming in Phase 2.

terminal.title: Web terminal
terminal.desc: Multi-tab PTY into the agent environment. Coming in Phase 4.

scheduler.title: Scheduled jobs
scheduler.desc: Manage cron jobs with natural-language task descriptions. Coming in Phase 2.

channels.title: Platform channels
channels.desc: Configure Telegram, Discord, Slack and 5 more. Coming in Phase 3.

models.title: Model providers
models.desc: Discover and manage models from your credential pool. Coming in Phase 2.

settings.title: Settings
settings.desc: Display, agent, memory, privacy, and server settings. Coming in Phase 2.

scheduler_page.title: Scheduled jobs
scheduler_page.subtitle: Run a prompt on a cron schedule. Each fire hits the Hermes adapter with your prompt as a single turn; the reply is stored only in the run history (no new chat session).
scheduler_page.empty_desc: Create one to run a prompt on a schedule. Use any 6-field cron expression — see the hint below the cron field.
scheduler_page.field_cron: Cron expression (sec min hour day month weekday)
scheduler_page.field_prompt_placeholder: Summarize yesterday's standup notes and list today's blockers.
scheduler_page.cron_hint: Example: '0 0 9 * * *' = every day at 09:00. Use '0 */5 * * * *' for every 5 minutes. Hermes also accepts 'every 2h', '30m', or ISO timestamps.
scheduler_page.runs_empty: No runs yet. Hermes will create them when the job fires.

consent.title: Access permission required
consent.desc: The app is trying to reach the path below, which sits outside your current workspace. Choose how to proceed:
